codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements: publishSignalEvent(), publishThread(), publishSnapshot() functions create AT Protocol records via com.atproto.repo.createRecord, return AT URIs, and support read-back via getRecord(). The worker added a well-structured getRecord() method and 10 vitest tests (exceeding the 6+ requirement). Lexicon schemas for all 3 required record types are defined. Code is clean, properly typed, and follows existing package patterns. Two non-blocking issues found: an unused import and a semantic mismatch in thread memberRefs format.

issues.description: LEXICON_GAUGE_DEFINITION is imported but never used anywhere in client.ts. Should be removed to keep imports clean.

toAtprotoThread() maps thread.members to memberRefs as local signal_event_id strings (e.g. 'sig-001'), but the lexicon (app.openhax.radar.thread.json) declares memberRefs items as format: 'at-uri'. The AT Protocol PDS doesn't validate record contents against lexicon schemas at write time, so this won't fail at runtime, but it's a semantic mismatch. In production, these would need to be resolved to actual AT URIs of published SignalEvent records. This is a design decision that should be documented.

getRecord() and deleteRecord() parse AT URIs with a simple string.replace('at://', '').split('/') approach. This handles valid AT URIs correctly but doesn't reject non-AT URIs that happen to contain 3+ path segments (e.g. 'http://evil.com/a/b/c' would pass the length check). The AT Protocol API would reject these downstream, so no functional bug, but more explicit validation (e.g. checking uri.startsWith('at://')) would be more defensive.

Publish methods use this.did (which defaults to '') without checking if the client is authenticated. If login() hasn't been called, createRecord will be called with repo: '', which will fail at the API level. A proactive guard like 'if (!this._did) throw new Error("Not authenticated")' would give a clearer error message. The test for unauthenticated publish works because the mock rejects the call, but in production the error message would be from the PDS, not application-level.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The toAtprotoThread() converter stores local IDs in memberRefs instead of AT URIs as declared by the lexicon schema. This design decision (local-IDs-first, resolve-to-AT-URIs-later) should be documented in architecture.md or the package README so future workers know whether to resolve signal_event_ids to AT URIs before publishing threads.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/signal-atproto/src/converters.ts:35 — memberRefs: thread.members.map((m) => m.signal_event_id) vs lexicons/app.openhax.radar.thread.json memberRefs items format: 'at-uri'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature description specifies 'Use BSKY_HANDLE and BSKY_APP_PASSWORD env vars for auth' but the client takes a config object (identifier/password) instead. This is actually good library design (env var reading belongs at the integration layer), but the convention for where env vars are read should be documented so future workers know the client doesn't auto-read env vars.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description vs packages/signal-atproto/src/client.ts:27 — SignalAtprotoConfig interface takes identifier/password, not env var names

summary: PASS. The at-protocol-publishing feature is well-implemented. All 3 required publish functions (publishSignalEvent, publishThread, publishSnapshot) serialize records correctly via converter functions, create records using com.atproto.repo.createRecord, and return AT URIs. The getRecord() read-back method correctly parses AT URIs and calls com.atproto.repo.getRecord. Lexicon schemas are defined for all 3 required record types plus 3 additional types. 10 vitest tests pass, exceeding the 6+ requirement, covering publish+read-back round-trips for all 3 types, auth failure, and getRecord error handling. TypeScript typecheck and build both succeed. Four non-blocking issues identified: unused import, memberRefs semantic format mismatch with lexicon, permissive AT URI validation, and missing pre-publish auth guard.
